The assignment

Questions & Sample Answers

Question 1

[1] "What were Thurston’s two key tasks going to school?"
 [1] To be a good person and to de-negrity himself to fit in at school.
 [2] It was “Don’t lose your blacknest.” and “Don’t lose your grasses.”
 [3]                                                                   
 [4]                                                                   
 [5] Do not lose your blackness and do not lose glasses.               
 [6] He had to protect his “blackness” and his glasses.                
 [7] Don’t lose his blackness and don’t lose his glasses.              
 [8] Not losing his blackness and his glasses.                         
 [9] Not to lose your blackness and your glasses.                      
[10] Don't lose your blackness and don't lose your glasses.            
9 Levels:  ... To be a good person and to de-negrity himself to fit in at school.

Question 2

[1] "Give an example of a newspaper story headline that Thurston mentions about 2018?"
 [1] White Woman Calls Cops On Black Woman Waiting For An Uber.                                                                           
 [2] “White Woman Calls Cops On Black Woman Waiting for an Uber”and “White Woman Calls Police On Eight-Year-Old Black Girl Selling Water".
 [3] “Police shoot another unarmed black person”                                                                                          
 [4] White woman calls the police on 8year old black girl selling water.                                                                  
 [5] White woman calls cops on black women waiting for an uber.                                                                           
 [6] Police shoot another unarmed black person.                                                                                           
 [7] Police shoot another unarmed black person.                                                                                           
 [8] White women call uvereats which is black women.                                                                                      
 [9] He talks about white people calling the police on Black people doing normal things, like having a BBQ.                               
[10] - white women calls cops on black women waiting for uber.                                                                            
9 Levels: - white women calls cops on black women waiting for uber. ...

Question 3

[1] "What is Thurston’s definition of “white supremacy”?"
 [1] It is a system of structure advantage and favours white people in social, political, and economic arenas.       
 [2] The situation that believes white people are better than others.                                                
 [3] - System of structural advantage.                                                                               
 [4] A system of structural advantage, and favors white people in social, political, and economic arenas.            
 [5]                                                                                                                 
 [6] It means a system of structural advantage and favors white people in social, political, and economic arenas.    
 [7] He says it is a system that helps white people more than others.                                                
 [8]                                                                                                                 
 [9] A system of structural advantage that favors white people over others in social, economic, and political arenas.
[10]                                                                                                                 
8 Levels:  ... The situation that believes white people are better than others.

Question 4:

[1] "How many levels does Thurston’s game of words have?"
 [1] It has four levels. The goal is to change the action in the headlines.
 [2] - three                                                               
 [3] It has four levels to show how the headlines change.                  
 [4]                                                                       
 [5]                                                                       
 [6] It has four levels.                                                   
 [7]                                                                       
 [8] A.His game has three levels and one final bonus level.                
 [9] Three levels and a bonus level.                                       
[10] 3 levels.                                                             
8 Levels:  - three ... Three levels and a bonus level.

Question 5:

[1] "How does Thurston conclude his talk?"
 [1] He concluded his speech that we need to change our actions and we can change it.                                                                               
 [2]                                                                                                                                                                
 [3]                                                                                                                                                                
 [4] He concludes by encouraging people to level up to use their choices and language to transform harmful stories into healing stories and create change in society
 [5] He asks us to use power to choose and to level up.                                                                                                             
 [6]                                                                                                                                                                
 [7] He concluded that we must change the action part. In addition, he suggested we see the structure for example, we see what it is.                               
 [8] He ends his talk by asking people to change their actions and society.                                                                                         
 [9] He says people should help make society better.                                                                                                                
[10] He concluded that changing the action is important.                                                                                                            
8 Levels:  ... He says people should help make society better.


Summaries

five sample summaries

[1]                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
[2] In his presentation, Baratunde Thurston talks about racism in the U.S though his experiences, feelings, real accident, and stories. He said that people treat black people as dangerous Black people when they are only doing normal things such as waiting for an Uber or selling water and so on. He also felt fear when a police officer stopped his car. He states that these problems are connected to white supremacy, which he defined as a system of structural advantage that favors white people over others in social, economic, and political arenas. Against this system, he creates a game called a game of words, that changes actions in this real accident. Through this game, he shows that people have better choices than calling the police. He believes that changing small actions can change society little by little. At the end of the talk,people can change society by changing their actions and the stories they create, and he asks us to level up.
[3] The speaker The speaker argued that you can break down any headlines into 3 elements: subject, action, target and activity. white people tend to be chosen as the subject, black people are often chosen as the target, and any slight behaviour can be the action in headlines. In other words, headlines typically portray                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      
[4] In this TED talk,Beratunde Thurston talks about racism in America and how language can reveal systems of discrimination. He explains how many news stories in 2018 showed white people calling the police such as waiting for an Uber,selling water,or having a barbecue.Thurston says these stories expose a system of white supremacy,which he defines as a structure that gives advantages to white people. To better understand these headlines, he created a game of words with several levels.First, people reverse the races in the headline.Next, they make the reversed story more believable.Finally, they change the harmful action into a positive one. Thurston believes changing action and stories can help change society itself. He ends by encouraging everyone to use their power to create a fairer and more inclusive world.                                                                                                                                 
[5] He talks about how to deconstruct racism. He looks at news headlines and finds a pattern: a subject takes an action against a target. In many stories, a white person calls the police on a black person for just living their lives. Thurston says white supremacy is a system of structure advantage that favors white people. He creates Thurston’s game to change these stories. He says we should change our actions. We can change the system by choosing better actions.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
5 Levels:  ...


Plagiarism Check

What was the longest string of words shared among the students’ summaries and transcript?
                                                                                        collocation
1 there were at least 4,400 documented racial terror lynchings of black people in the united states
2             from sitting in a starbucks to using the wrong type of barbecue to napping to walking
  count
1     2
2     2
Comment: The first one is a memorable chunk of English that you may not need to paraphrase.

Not plagiarism

Comment: The second one is a bit rambling. Normally we wouldn’t remember his words exactly. It’s either slowly dictated, or copied from the transcript.

Plagiarism

word count for summary


Opinions

five sample opinions

[1]                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 
[2] ： I think there is less discrimination in Japan than in the U.S. This is probably because Japan has fewer ethnic groups. To be honest, I sometimes feel scared when I see Black people in Japan. I do not know why I feel this way. However, I hope that in the future, we will stop judging people just by their skin color or where they come from.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
[3] I thought this TED talk was very powerful and creative.Turston used humor and word games to explain serious issues about racism and inequality in a way that was easy to understand.I especially liked how he showed that changing language can help people recognize unfair systems in society. His example of headlines were shocking because the Black people in those stories were simply doing normal activities.The game of words was interesting because it encouraged the audiences to think critically and imagine better choices instead of harmful reactions.I also liked his final message that everyone has the power to change society through their actions. This talk made me think more deeply about how racism can appear in everyday situations and how impact empathy and understanding are.
[4] I think this story was very important, especially now, when the movement to eliminate immigrants is becoming stronger. As he says, in recent years, we often see news about people being judged by their race and treated unfairly. It is scary that innocent people are sometimes killed because racial prejudice. After watching this video, I think it is important for us to understand each other and kind words to make society better.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
[5] I thought this talk was very interesting and powerful. Even though the topic was serious, Thurston used humor, so it was easy to understand. I was surprised that many Black people are treated unfairly in everyday life. This talk made me realize that racism is not only about bad people, but also about systems in society. I also liked how he used a word game to explain difficult ideas. It made me think about how important our choices and actions are. After watching this talk, I wanted to learn more about racism and social problems.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         
5 Levels:  ...
